purpose: User-invocable forked-context Opus skill (`!p_track`) — the PRIMARY swarm orchestration skill. 3 phases / 17 steps: Phase 1 Planning (Steps 1-11: resolve project_root, read master instructions, parse prompt + slug, deep analysis, read context files + build convention_map, decompose tasks 1-5 min / 1-2 files modified, determine Waves vs Chains, write plan.json with full schema (context.{prompt, project_root, tracker_root, dashboard_id, type, directories, conventions, reference_code, architectural_decisions, edge_cases, shared_constraints} + tasks[].{id, title, wave, depends_on, directory, description, approach, files, context, critical, success_criteria, instruction_mode, tags}), verify dependencies (toposort, critical path, no orphans), select dashboard + write plan.json BEFORE initialization.json BEFORE logs.json, then Approval Gate Step 11E — write 'Plan ready for review' permission entry, halt, await user, write 'Approval granted' info entry); Phase 2 Execution (initial dispatch, FULL/LITE worker prompt construction with full template including TEMPLATE_VERSION:p_track_v2, eager dispatch on completions, failure recovery); Phase 3 Completion (Steps 17A-F: finalize task file, append log, post-swarm verification, compute metrics, NON-NEGOTIABLE final report, save to history). Includes the canonical worker prompt template.
gotchas: Write order is HOOK-ENFORCED: plan.json -> initialization.json -> logs.json -> Approval Gate -> dispatch
Approval Gate is hook-enforced (validate-approval-gate.sh) AND prose-NON-NEGOTIABLE — bypass is impossible
FULL vs LITE worker prompt: FULL for upstream deps / 3+ files / coordination / high risk; LITE for simple/independent/single-file/mechanical
TEMPLATE_VERSION: p_track_v2 in worker prompts is the marker validate-worker-prompt.sh checks
Worker prompt template has 11 required sections (see validate-worker-prompt.sh full set)
Context budget: ~800 lines / 8000 tokens max per worker prompt — bloat causes context exhaustion
Sweet spot: 4-8 tasks per swarm; merge back if splitting doesn't reduce critical path 20%+
Plan must reflect REAL deep-thinking — `approach` field must not be a restatement of `description`
context: fork — runs in a forked Claude context; model: opus
patterns: Eager dispatch (waves are visual, dependencies drive)
FULL/LITE prompt mode selection by task profile
conventions: Step 11E is the Approval Gate sub-step
Worker prompts use ═══ box markers for section separation
